behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module handoff boundary (history manager -> LLM client payload), not just pure string logic. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; a stub LLM client captures sent payload. LLM-DEP: assertion is plumbing-only, not output quality. IA-UA demotion proof: behavior text says "history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages"; this checks request construction, not model judgment. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic/Workflow because no requirement about response quality or multi-step model decisions; rejected System because no real infra is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Wire conversation service + history trimmer + LLM client adapter with a spy/stub client; feed >10-message history and assert the outbound LLM request contains exactly the latest 10 messages in order.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model output relevance/groundedness, which is non-deterministic and quality-based. A stub would always hide this failure mode. MULTI-STEP: this behavior is about one generation decision, not a multi-agent chain, so Agentic (not Workflow). Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly evaluates output quality: "addresses the customer's question" vs "generic or off-topic reply."
behaviors.test_description: Run the real generation call on a curated prompt set with relevance rubrics (or human/LLM grader), and fail when responses are generic/off-topic beyond threshold.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: "correctly identifies" is model-judgment quality, not deterministic plumbing. MULTI-STEP: requires sequential agentic stages (initial response -> tone evaluation -> regeneration trigger -> regenerated response), so Workflow is the minimum boundary. MOCK-HIDE: mocked tone checker or mocked generations can pass while real tone judgments fail. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic because behavior explicitly includes both decision and downstream action: "identifies ... and triggers regeneration," i.e., multi-step sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end with real model calls on casual-tier scenarios designed to elicit overly formal first drafts; verify the tone check flags them and that a regeneration step is actually invoked and recorded.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears in service->repository state handoff and transaction semantics, so beyond unit. REAL-INFRA: local DB integration is sufficient; no real network/OS/subprocess dependency is required. LLM-DEP: not evaluating model quality, only persistence fields and ordering. IA-UA demotion proof: behavior text is "persisted to the database with correct timestamps"; this is storage plumbing, not AI output correctness. Rejection gate: rejected System because real external infra is unnecessary; rejected Agentic/Workflow because assertions are DB record completeness/timestamps, not model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Use service + repository with a real local test DB; execute a flow including regeneration events and assert persisted rows contain all exchange artifacts with correct timestamp ordering and linkage.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is orchestration/state handoff across modules (draft lifecycle, replacement, response presenter). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP demotion: condition is pre-set by behavior text; correctness being tested is visibility/state replacement. IA-UA demotion proof: exact text "When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft" makes the trigger a precondition and checks delivery plumbing only. INTEGRATION-BRAKE(d): although LLM-involved stages exist, this behavior does not test model decision quality; it tests post-trigger replacement semantics. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic/Workflow because no assertion about whether the model made the right tone decision or produced better content, only which draft is exposed.
behaviors.test_description: In an integrated service test, force/fixture the tone-check result to trigger regeneration, run two-response flow, and assert customer-facing output and outbound channel contain only the second draft while first draft is retained only in internal logs if applicable.
